strategy: Ra is a Rank 4 Balance boss with only 3,000 health behind a wooden skeleton key, meant for level-20-ish wizards. An overleveled wizard ends this almost instantly, so bring a strong hitter and just plan around the auras.
Bring a balance dispel. Ra opens by interrupt-casting Balance of Power and recasts it any time you try to replace the Global, so a dispel is the only clean way to shut it off.
Wear high-accuracy gear or pack Precision. Ra randomly throws Black Mantle or a +10% Precision that can hit either side, so accuracy insurance keeps your setup from fizzling.

Plan burst around the aura cycle. At the end of every fourth round Ra interrupt-casts an aura on everyone: Amplify first (round 4), then Fortify (round 8), alternating. Try to land your big hit on an Amplify round for free extra damage.

Hit with Myth, Life, or Death (+10% incoming) and avoid Balance (30% resist). Clear the three minions (Sia ~920, Hu ~800, Heka ~880) with an AoE alongside your hit on Ra, and carry heals for the low-level version of the fight.
cheatNotes: At the very start Ra interrupt-casts Balance of Power as a Global. A balance dispel removes it.
If you try to swap out his Global with your own, Ra just interrupt-recasts Balance of Power. Dispel it rather than trying to overwrite it.
At random, Ra casts Black Mantle or a +10% Precision that can land on either all allies or all enemies, so your accuracy may drop unexpectedly. Bring accuracy gear.
If you Spy Ra during that accuracy cheat, only the plain versions of Precision or Black Mantle are shown, hiding the +10% detail.

At the end of every fourth round Ra interrupt-casts an aura on everyone, starting with Amplify (round 4), then Fortify (round 8), then alternating. Amplify rounds boost damage for both sides, so time your hit there.
